name: Reverse a sequence (in-place, no stdlib reverse)

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_rev/rev.py — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T. Each case: line A is N (>= 0). Line B is N space-separated integers (or empty if N=0). For each case, output the integers in REVERSED order, space-separated on one line (or empty line for N=0). Implement the reversal yourself — do NOT call any stdlib reverse helper. Pure Python; no numpy.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_rev; sed 's/#.*//' rev.py | grep -qE "\\.reverse[[:space:]]*\\(|(^|[^A-Za-z0-9_.])reversed[[:space:]]*\\(|\\[[[:space:]]*::[[:space:]]*-1[[:space:]]*\\]" && { echo 'forbidden api'; exit 1; }; python3 rev.py < input.txt > out.txt; diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_rev/rev.go (package main). Reads from stdin: line 1 is T. Each case: line A is N (>= 0). Line B is N space-separated integers (or empty if N=0). For each case, output the integers in REVERSED order, space-separated on one line (or empty line for N=0). Implement the reversal yourself — do NOT call any stdlib reverse helper. Compile with `go build -o rev rev.go`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_rev; sed 's#//.*##' rev.go | grep -qE "slices\\.Reverse|sort\\.Reverse" && { echo 'forbidden api'; exit 1; }; go build -o rev rev.go && ./rev < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_rev/rev.c — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T. Each case: line A is N (>= 0). Line B is N space-separated integers (or empty if N=0). For each case, output the integers in REVERSED order, space-separated on one line (or empty line for N=0). Implement the reversal yourself — do NOT call any stdlib reverse helper. Compile with `gcc -O2 -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -o rev rev.c -lm`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_rev; gcc -O2 -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -o rev rev.c -lm && ./rev < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_rev/rev.cpp — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T. Each case: line A is N (>= 0). Line B is N space-separated integers (or empty if N=0). For each case, output the integers in REVERSED order, space-separated on one line (or empty line for N=0). Implement the reversal yourself — do NOT call any stdlib reverse helper. Compile with `g++ -O2 -std=c++17 -Wall -Wextra -o rev rev.cpp`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_rev; sed 's#//.*##' rev.cpp | grep -qE "std::reverse|reverse_iterator" && { echo 'forbidden api'; exit 1; }; g++ -O2 -std=c++17 -Wall -Wextra -o rev rev.cpp && ./rev < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_rev/rev.rs — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T. Each case: line A is N (>= 0). Line B is N space-separated integers (or empty if N=0). For each case, output the integers in REVERSED order, space-separated on one line (or empty line for N=0). Implement the reversal yourself — do NOT call any stdlib reverse helper. Idiomatic Rust 2021+; compile with `rustc -O rev.rs -o rev`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_rev; sed 's#//.*##' rev.rs | grep -qE "\\.reverse[[:space:]]*\\(|\\.rev[[:space:]]*\\(" && { echo 'forbidden api'; exit 1; }; rustc -O rev.rs -o rev && ./rev < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_rev/rev.zig — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T. Each case: line A is N (>= 0). Line B is N space-separated integers (or empty if N=0). For each case, output the integers in REVERSED order, space-separated on one line (or empty line for N=0). Implement the reversal yourself — do NOT call any stdlib reverse helper. Use Zig 0.16+ idioms. Entrypoint: `pub fn main(init: std.process.Init) !void`. For stdio, the rich API (`takeDelimiter`, `appendRemainingUnlimited`, `print`, `writeAll`, `flush`) lives on the `std.Io.Reader`/`Writer` *interface*, NOT on the concrete `std.Io.File.Reader`/`Writer`. Reach the interface via `&fr.interface`. Canonical setup: `var in_buf: [4096]u8 = undefined; var fr = std.Io.File.stdin().reader(init.io, &in_buf); const r = &fr.interface;` and the symmetric writer. Then `try r.takeDelimiter('\n')` (returns `?[]u8`, null at EOF) for line-by-line, or `try r.appendRemainingUnlimited(init.arena.allocator(), &list)` to slurp. Always `try w.flush()` before return — output is buffered. Strict-default footguns (errors, not warnings, in 0.16): rename unused params to `_`, discard unused locals with `_ = x;`, prefer `const` over `var` when not mutated. Use `std.Io` (capital I), not `std.io`. Compile with `zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast rev.zig`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_rev; sed 's#//.*##' rev.zig | grep -qE "std\\.mem\\.reverse" && { echo 'forbidden api'; exit 1; }; zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast rev.zig && ./rev < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK
